summary: ANDROID 🟥 | FMD | Play/Pause button giving different outcomes
scenario: Rapidly clicking Play in Find My Device causes tones to stack into noise with no Pause button, requiring device power-off
description_clean: In the Find My Device (FMD) feature, pressing the Play/Pause button on a connected device produces inconsistent behavior. When pressing Play then Stop then Play again, the tone cannot be heard. Additionally, rapidly clicking Play can cause tones to 'stack' into very bad noise, with no Pause button available, forcing the user to turn off the device. This issue is reproducible on every device when clicking quickly.
steps: 1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app on the connected smartphone.
2. Navigate to Home screen and select the connected Poly Bluetooth audio device.
3. Go to Device Settings → Find My Device (FMD).
4. Tap the Play button to start the FMD tone.
5. Tap the Stop button to stop the FMD tone.
6. Tap the Play button again to replay the FMD tone.
7. Observe whether the tone is audible on the second Play.
8. Tap Play rapidly multiple times in quick succession.
9. Observe the audio output behavior during rapid clicking.
expected: The FMD tone should play clearly each time Play is pressed, and stop cleanly when Stop is pressed. Rapid clicking should not cause audio artifacts or stacking of tones. A Pause button should be available to allow stopping the tone without turning off the device.
actual: After pressing Play → Stop → Play, the second Play produces no audible tone. Rapid clicking causes tones to stack into very bad noise, and there is no Pause button available, forcing the user to turn off the device to stop the noise.
